observation.summary: Runtime secret release evidence needs a public contract without exposing private token values.

hypothesis.summary: A metadata-only packet contract can prove that public CI never requires raw secret collection paths.

execution.summary: Validate the contract, generate the reader doc, and publish sanitized public evidence JSON.

evaluation.summary: The verifier rejects raw packet fields, secret read/decrypt AWS operations, missing generated docs, and missing forbidden operation coverage.

retrospective.summary: Private infra remains the live evidence owner, while public daemon only owns the executable metadata boundary.

evidence.proves: runtime secret evidence boundary generates sanitized public evidence
public CI uploads sanitized runtime secret evidence artifacts without private secret access
